label: Causal inference

node.label: Causal inference
node.definition: Structural causal models, interventions, counterfactuals, and confounding distinguish correlation from causal effect.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Causal inference is to connect Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Causal representation learning, Knowledge editing & memory consolidation, Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis.
examples: substrate for Knowledge editing & memory consolidation
substrate for Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.

dependencies.rationale: Causal inference rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
Causal inference rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Statistics & experimental design.
Causal inference rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.
